<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try a different approach - rotate the character to face up and move it to the green frame
# First, let me understand the character's orientation by looking at the shape

# To face up, I need to rotate clockwise (SPACE)
# Let me try SPACE to rotate

action(['SPACE'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After SPACE:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R']:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check if character is now facing up
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter area (rows 11-25, cols 14-28):")
for i in range(11, 26):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][11:31]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

    if n['color'] in ['W', 'R']:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check if character is now facing up
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter area (rows 11-25, cols 14-28):")
for i in range(11, 26):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {lines[i][11:31]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
